own_review.point: polyglot-common bumped to 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT, an unreleased version that depends on the unmerged upstream PR takari/polyglot-maven#242; the dependency is unresolvable and the PR cannot be merged/released as-is (author admits it)

Correctness of the IS_FIRST scan depends entirely on the NEW polyglot behavior (try mappings in priority order, stop at first locatePom hit); with released 0.4.8 semantics (all mappings probed for every dir) the aggregator scan would fire on the first probed directory regardless of its type, potentially generating pom.tycho inside bundle/target dirs

ThreadLocal state leak: IS_FIRST.set(false) happens unconditionally, but IS_FIRST.remove() only runs inside 'if (subFolders != null)'; if dir.listFiles() returns null (not a directory / IO error) or the scan loop throws, the thread-local stays false forever and all subsequent aggregator directories on that thread are silently never scanned; remove() must be in a finally block

Behavior regression: nested aggregator directories (e.g. releng/products where products is itself only an aggregator) were previously detected via COMMON_NAMES at any depth; now nested folders are never scanned (IS_FIRST=false in recursive calls), so such modules are silently dropped from the build

Removal of the documented tycho.pomless.aggregator.names system property is a breaking change that is silently ignored if users still set it; no RELEASE_NOTES.md entry, although the repo convention documents such removals (cf. tycho.pomless.testbundle removal entry)

Flaky integration test: assertMentionedDirectories asserts exact list equality ['target','otherFolder','target'] for skipped dirs, but their order comes from File.listFiles() which has no ordering guarantee (filesystem-dependent)
Static ThreadLocal as a recursion guard is fragile design; an explicit reentrancy flag/parameter would be clearer and not risk cross-invocation leakage in long-lived JVMs
Unrelated test weakening: testPomlessTestPluginDetection goal changed from 'test' to 'compile', so test-plugin execution is no longer exercised (the removed verifyTextInLog("") was a no-op)
Removing the getPriority() default from public AbstractTychoMapping is source-incompatible for any external subclass of this public extension class
Path.of(location) can throw unchecked InvalidPathException for unusual location strings; also inconsistent: TychoTargetMapping/TychoRepositoryMapping still use String.endsWith
Typos: 'that the the pom.xml reader' in TychoFeatureMapping priority comment; 'scaned' variable in PomlessTest; 'structre' in test data

points.point: Compilation error: TychoBundleMapping does not override getPriority()
points.why: The diff explicitly adds getPriority() returning +2 to TychoBundleMapping

points.point: Feature/Repository/Target all inherit priority 1 from AbstractXMLTychoMapping, contradicting the PR description
points.why: The diff adds distinct overrides: Feature +1, Repository -1, Target -2

points.point: ThreadLocal leak: remove() only inside 'if (subFolders != null)'; listFiles()==null leaves it false forever; use try/finally
points.why: Exactly the real leak path, correct mechanism and correct fix

points.point: maven.config still contains -Dtycho.pomless.aggregator.names line; remove it
points.why: The PR diff already removes that exact line from .mvn/maven.config

points.point: Blocking SNAPSHOT dependency polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT, cannot merge until released
points.why: Verified merge/release blocker; depends on unmerged upstream PR

missed: nested aggregator directories regression (silently dropped modules)
removed property is a breaking change with no RELEASE_NOTES.md entry
human's root cause: polyglot XmlMapping.accept() matches any *.xml (feature.xml) so feature priority must beat it

points.point: IS_FIRST only removed when !modules.isEmpty(); empty-modules first scan leaks false permanently
points.why: remove() is placed after the for-loop BEFORE the modules.isEmpty() check; the empty-modules path does reset; the real leak is subFolders==null

points.point: No reset when getPrimaryArtifact returns null; reset in finally or session-scoped state
points.why: The finally-based reset is the correct fix for the genuine subFolders==null/exception leak path

points.point: IS_FIRST not thread-safe under -T; concurrent scans and races on pom.tycho
points.why: Speculative: module/pom discovery happens during single-threaded reactor graph building; ThreadLocal per-thread behavior is by design

points.point: Bundle falls back to Mapping interface default priority, possibly below Feature's inherited 1
points.why: The diff adds explicit getPriority()=+2 to TychoBundleMapping and distinct overrides to the XML mappings

points.point: Removed constant; config line removed in this PR; verify no other references to the property
points.why: Vague 'verify elsewhere' advice; no concrete issue (no other references exist)

points.point: Path.of(location).endsWith is more robust; ensure location never null
points.why: Praise of the diff; accept() already null-checks location before isValidLocation

points.point: polyglot-common 0.4.9-SNAPSHOT unreleased, upstream PR must be merged; blocking dependency
points.why: Verified merge/release blocker

missed: nested aggregator directories regression (silently dropped modules)
removed property is a breaking change with no RELEASE_NOTES.md entry
human's root cause: polyglot XmlMapping.accept() matches any *.xml (feature.xml) so feature priority must beat it

points.point: IS_FIRST pattern broken: remove() reinitializes to true, defeating scan-once purpose
points.why: Misreads intent: the flag is a recursion guard during one scan; resetting to true afterwards is exactly the intended behavior (each aggregator dir must be scanned)

points.point: TychoBundleMapping has no getPriority() override; possible compile error or wrong default
points.why: The diff adds getPriority()=+2 to TychoBundleMapping

points.point: Feature/Repository/Target inherit identical priority 1; ordering undefined
points.why: The diff adds distinct overrides +1/-1/-2 to those three mappings

points.point: maven.config still contains the stale -Dtycho.pomless.aggregator.names line; remove it
points.why: The PR diff already removes that line

points.point: SNAPSHOT dependency on unreleased polyglot-common; will fail to resolve until upstream PR is released
points.why: Verified merge/release blocker

missed: ThreadLocal leak on subFolders==null/exception path (remove() not in finally)
nested aggregator directories regression (silently dropped modules)
removed property is a breaking change with no RELEASE_NOTES.md entry
human's root cause: polyglot XmlMapping.accept() matches any *.xml (feature.xml) so feature priority must beat it

points.point: CRITICAL: IS_FIRST only allows the FIRST aggregator directory ever encountered to be scanned; fundamental logic error
points.why: IS_FIRST.remove() after the scan loop resets it to true, so each subsequent aggregator dir is scanned; the PR's own IT asserts two aggregators are scanned

points.point: Leak when modules is empty: remove() only inside if(!modules.isEmpty())
points.why: remove() is before the modules.isEmpty() check; the empty case does reset; real leak is subFolders==null

points.point: ThreadLocal under -T: threads scan 'their own first aggregators', non-deterministic, multiple pom.tycho files
points.why: Built on the false 'only first scanned' premise; discovery is single-threaded and per-dir pom.tycho content does not vary by thread

points.point: Test has three aggregator dirs but IS_FIRST allows one; test will likely fail
points.why: False: bundles-2 has an explicit pom.xml, and the reset makes both real aggregators scan; the IT passes by design

points.point: Priority inversion: Bundle loses explicit priority, falls below XML mappings' 1
points.why: The diff adds getPriority()=+2 to TychoBundleMapping and distinct overrides to Feature/Repository/Target

points.point: Path.of(...).endsWith fixes false-match (mypom.tycho); add a unit test for it
points.why: Correctly identifies the semantic change and asks for concrete test coverage of it

missed: SNAPSHOT/unreleased polyglot-common dependency is a merge blocker (not mentioned at all)
ThreadLocal leak on subFolders==null/exception path (their leak claim has the wrong mechanism)
nested aggregator directories regression (silently dropped modules)
removed property is a breaking change with no RELEASE_NOTES.md entry
human's root cause: polyglot XmlMapping.accept() matches any *.xml (feature.xml) so feature priority must beat it

qualitative: v2 is the best of a weak field: it flags the SNAPSHOT-dependency merge blocker and gives the correct fix (reset the ThreadLocal in finally) for the genuine state-leak, with only two fabrications. v1 is close behind — it is the only candidate to describe the real leak mechanism (listFiles()==null) precisely and also flags the blocker, but it fabricates three claims (missing Bundle getPriority 'compilation error', non-distinct XML priorities, stale maven.config line) that are flatly contradicted by the actual diff, which adds those overrides and removes that line. v3 repeats all three of those fabrications plus a fourth (claiming the remove()-resets-to-true behavior 'defeats' the design, when it is the intended recursion-guard reset), salvaged only by the blocker point. v4 is worst: its entire review is built on misreading the IS_FIRST lifecycle ('only the first aggregator is ever scanned'), cascades that error into four follow-on points, and completely misses the SNAPSHOT blocker. No candidate caught the nested-aggregator regression, the missing RELEASE_NOTES entry for the removed tycho.pomless.aggregator.names property, the order-dependent IT assertion, or the human's root-cause analysis of polyglot's XmlMapping.accept() matching feature.xml.
